title: Workspace-Comms A2A Envelope Contract (message/send)
description: SSOT JSON-Schema (draft 2020-12) for the A2A JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope proxied through POST /workspaces/:id/a2a. Promoted from workspace-comms/registry-contract.md, DERIVED FROM THE WIRE AUTHORITY: molecule-core workspace-server/internal/handlers/a2a_proxy.go (the message/send normalization + the synthetic `{status:"queued"}` ack) and registry.go buildConciergeWarmupBody (the canonical message/send body the platform itself constructs). The instance models BOTH directions: `request` = the A2A v0.3 `message/send` JSON-RPC request, `response` = the platform's queued ack. LOAD-BEARING INVARIANT (A2A v0.3): each `params.message.parts[]` item is discriminated by `kind` (NOT `type`) — a `type`-keyed Part is silently dropped by the receiver's v0.3 Pydantic validator (#2345); text parts carry `kind:"text"` + `text`. The producer wraps a bare body in the JSON-RPC envelope and injects messageId if missing, but the canonical/produced shape is the full envelope modeled here. The push-async ack is what THIS proxy returns for poll-mode / over-budget turns; a synchronous push turn instead streams back the upstream agent's own JSON-RPC result (passthrough, not owned by this contract — see response.note).

request.description: A2A v0.3 JSON-RPC 2.0 message/send request body.

jsonrpc.description: JSON-RPC version. Always "2.0" (the producer injects it when a bare body omits it).

id.description: JSON-RPC request id. A fresh UUID per turn (the producer injects one if absent) so the turn is idempotent at the runtime and never collides with a real message.

method.description: JSON-RPC method. The canonical workspace-comms verb is `message/send` (the only method the can_delegate gate and the queued-ack path special-case). Modeled as a non-empty string; the queued ack echoes this value back.

params.description: message/send params. Carries the single `message` object.

message.description: The A2A message. role/messageId/parts are the load-bearing fields.

role.description: Message role. The platform-constructed turns use "user".

messageId.description: Stable per-turn message id (the producer ensures one exists — required by a2a-sdk). Distinct from the JSON-RPC `id`, though the platform sets them equal for its own warmup/delegate turns.

parts.description: Ordered message parts. Each part is discriminated by `kind` (A2A v0.3). A text part is `{"kind":"text","text":"..."}`. NEVER `type` — a type-keyed part is dropped by the v0.3 validator (#2345).

kind.description: Part discriminator (A2A v0.3). "text" for a text part; other kinds (file/data) extend this. MUST be `kind`, not `type`.

response.description: The platform's synthetic push-async / poll-mode ACK returned by the A2A proxy (HTTP 200). NOTE: a synchronous push turn instead returns the upstream agent's own JSON-RPC result envelope (passthrough), which is the remote agent's shape, not owned by this contract; only the queued ack is the platform's own product here.

status.description: The literal "queued" — delivery acknowledged but pending consumption (poll-mode logged to activity, or push over-budget acked async).

delivery_mode.description: How the queued turn will be delivered: "push-async" (turn exceeded the sync budget, will finish out-of-band) or "poll" (recorded to activity_logs for the poll-mode agent to pull).

method.description: Echo of the request `method` (e.g. "message/send") the ack corresponds to.
